About.title: About this website
About.description: The <aip>AIP of Belgium & Luxembourg</aip> is available as a free version. This website aims to simplify the search for airfields, as the basic version provided by skeyes does not include a search function. We do not accept any liability for the accuracy or timeliness of the AIP (Aeronautical Information Publication), as it is not published by us. We only provide convenient links to the respective approach charts.

Common.homeLink: AIP:Aero - Aeronautical Information Publications (AIP) & approach charts for Europe
Common.installHint: Install as an app: open your browser's Share menu and choose 'Add to Home Screen'. Then open the app once while online and save your fields there again - the app has its own offline storage.
Common.installHintMac: On a Mac: install via Safari's Share menu using 'Add to Dock'. Then open the app once while online and save your fields there again - the app has its own offline storage.
Common.bulkNoSpace: Not enough free storage on this device
Common.bulkScope: Included: all airfield pages with aerodrome data. Not included: approach chart PDFs (can be saved per airfield) and live weather (needs a connection).
Common.favoritesEmpty: No favorites yet - save an airfield offline on its detail page and it will appear here.
Common.recentlyViewedEmpty: Airfields you open will appear here automatically for quick access.
Common.aipLoginHint: A login or registration may be required.
Common.noChartPdf: No separate chart PDF is published for this aerodrome - the AIP button above opens its official aerodrome entry.

TermsPage.title: Terms of Service
TermsPage.intro: AIP:Aero is a free search and index service for Aeronautical Information Publications (AIP), approach charts and airport data across Europe.
TermsPage.servicesText: <home>AIP:Aero</home> lets you find aerodromes by name or ICAO code and takes you directly to the official AIP publication of the respective country. In addition, our airport pages embed supplementary information such as weather reports (METAR/TAF), aerodrome data (runways, frequencies, elevation), maps and contact details, and pages can be saved for offline use. The service is provided free of charge and without registration.
TermsPage.disclaimerText: AIP:Aero cannot be held liable under any circumstances. We do not publish aeronautical documents ourselves: we only link to the latest Aeronautical Information Publication (AIP) made available by the official provider of the respective country. The same applies to weather data (METAR/TAF) and airport data: we embed this information on our pages, but it originates from the external sources listed below. We assume no liability or warranty for the accuracy, completeness or timeliness of any linked or embedded content, nor for the availability of this website. This website does not replace the official pre-flight briefing: the pilot in command remains solely responsible for obtaining and verifying all information required for a flight.
TermsPage.sourcesIntro: We obtain the linked and embedded data from the following sources (addresses shown as plain text, deliberately not clickable):
TermsPage.sourcesAip: Official AIP publications we link to
TermsPage.srcOpenAip: extended aerodrome data: runways, frequencies, fuel
TermsPage.metaTitle: Terms of Service - AIP:Aero Belgium & Luxembourg
TermsPage.metaDescription: Terms of service of AIP:Aero, the free AIP and approach chart search for Belgium & Luxembourg and Europe: service description, disclaimer of liability and data sources.

EfbPage.title: AIP:Aero pilot tools: EFB app, offline charts, imports and data
EfbPage.intro: How to install AIP:Aero on your EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) tablet, save approach charts offline, download country packs and hand fields over to your planning tools - step by step.
EfbPage.metaTitle: EFB Guide Belgium & Luxembourg - AIP & approach charts on your tablet | AIP:Aero
EfbPage.metaDescription: Install AIP:Aero as an app on your EFB tablet, save approach charts offline, download whole countries and import chart PDFs into ForeFlight, SkyDemon & Co (Belgium & Luxembourg).
EfbPage.installText: AIP:Aero is an installable web app: it opens full-screen like a native app and everything you save is available offline. No app store needed.
EfbPage.installIos: iPad / iPhone (Safari): open aip.aero, tap the share icon, then "Add to Home Screen".
EfbPage.installAndroid: Android (Chrome): open aip.aero, tap the menu, then "Add to home screen" / "Install app".
EfbPage.installDesktop: Desktop (Chrome/Edge): click the install icon in the address bar - or use the button below. macOS (Safari): Share icon, then "Add to Dock".
EfbPage.offlineText: Every airport detail page has a "Save offline" button. It stores the page and, where the AIP publishes one, the approach-chart PDF on your device; the field then appears under Favorites on the country page. Saved copies show their download date.
EfbPage.bulkText: On the <list>airport list</list> you can download all of a country's detail pages in one go (pages only, without chart PDFs - save those per field). The pack shows its size before downloading and can be updated or removed at any time.
EfbPage.favoritesText: Fields you save for offline use automatically appear as favorites, and the last airfields you opened are listed under "Recently viewed" - both are stored only on your device, no account needed. You will find both lists <country>on the country page</country>.
EfbPage.importTitle: Chart PDFs in ForeFlight, SkyDemon & Co.
EfbPage.importText: Where the national AIP publishes chart PDFs, the chart box on the detail page links the current PDF including its AIRAC date. Download it and import it into your EFB app as custom content, e.g. ForeFlight (Custom Content), SkyDemon (Custom Airfield Plates) or Garmin Pilot. Germany publishes HTML pages instead of PDFs - use the AIP link there.
EfbPage.toolsText: The location box on every detail page opens the same field directly in SkyVector, Windy and autorouter - handy for route planning, weather models and flight-plan filing.
EfbPage.weatherText: Detail pages show live METAR/TAF with plain-language decoding, the flight-category badge and a per-runway head- and crosswind breakdown - a quick cross-check, not a substitute for your official briefing.
EfbPage.customsText: Detail pages show whether a field offers customs clearance; where a national AIP lists it in GEN 1.2, the value is verified against that official source. For border-crossing paperwork the location box links the official forms (e.g. the UK General Aviation Report), and the map can filter for customs airfields. Rules change - always confirm with the current AIP and the local authority.
EfbPage.disclaimerText: AIP:Aero links official AIP publications but is not an official source. Offline copies are marked with their download date and can become outdated with every AIRAC cycle - verify chart currency before every flight and always obtain an official pre-flight briefing.
EfbPage.apiText: For developers and EFB providers: AIP:Aero's structured aerodrome data (the normalized aerodrome index, chart-PDF deep links, AIRAC cycles, coordinates, runways, frequencies and customs information) is available via API for integrations. We are open for cooperations; <contact>get in touch</contact>.
EfbPage.faq1a: Saved airfields and downloaded country packs work without internet; every offline copy shows a banner with its save date. Live weather (METAR/TAF) always needs a connection.
EfbPage.faq2a: All countries currently live are listed on the <home>homepage</home> - the coverage grows continuously. Each country card shows the available chart types.
EfbPage.faq3a: Chart links point to the official national AIP publications, including the AIRAC date where published. Always verify against the official source before flight.

CountryFaq.title: Frequently asked questions
CountryFaq.q1: What is the AIP Belgium & Luxembourg?
CountryFaq.a1: The AIP (Aeronautical Information Publication) is the official aeronautical manual of Belgium & Luxembourg, published by skeyes: aerodrome data, procedures and approach charts, amended in the 28-day AIRAC cycle. AIP:Aero links you straight to the official publication.
CountryFaq.a2: Yes - skeyes publishes the AIP of Belgium & Luxembourg including <list>approach charts</list> free of charge, and AIP:Aero is free too: no account, no subscription.
CountryFaq.q3: Which charts and airfields are available for Belgium & Luxembourg?
CountryFaq.a3: For Belgium & Luxembourg you will find <vfr>VFR airfields</vfr>, <ifr>IFR airports</ifr>, <heli>heliports</heli> and <mil>military aerodromes</mil> - each with a direct link to the official chart, decoded weather (METAR/TAF) and aerodrome data.
CountryFaq.q4: Can I use AIP:Aero offline or on my EFB?
CountryFaq.a4: Yes - AIP:Aero installs as an app (PWA), and single aerodromes or the whole country can be saved offline. See the <efb>EFB guide</efb>.
